_comment2: Audiense Customer Data Mart - RDS PostgreSQL 16 Parameter Group
_comment3: Instance: db.m6g.large (2 vCPU, 8GB RAM) — small scale data mart
_comment4: Scale: ~20k customers, ~50k prospects, activity history (<1M rows total)
_comment5: Apply BEFORE running 01_datamart_init.sql. Reboot required after creation.
description: Audiense data mart - analytical workload on db.m6g.large (2vCPU / 8GB)

parameters.name: shared_preload_libraries
parameters._note: Required for pg_stat_statements. This is the only parameter that needs a reboot - do this first.

parameters.name: shared_buffers
parameters._note: 25% of RAM = ~2GB on m6g.large. RDS formula syntax handles this automatically regardless of instance class - do not hardcode.

parameters.name: effective_cache_size
parameters._note: 75% of RAM = ~6GB. Planner hint only - no memory is allocated. Tells the planner how much OS page cache is likely available.

parameters.name: work_mem
parameters._note: 16MB per sort/hash operation. Conservative for 8GB RAM. With 50 connections × 2 sorts = 100 × 16MB = 1.6GB peak - safe headroom. Increase to 32768 (32MB) if you see temp files in logs and connection count is low.

parameters.name: maintenance_work_mem
parameters._note: 128MB for VACUUM, CREATE INDEX, ALTER TABLE. At this data scale, index builds will complete in seconds regardless.

parameters.name: max_connections
parameters._note: Conservative for m6g.large. Matches sum of all CONNECTION LIMIT values in init SQL (5+5+15+10=35) plus headroom for admin connections. Each idle connection uses ~5MB RAM on an 8GB instance.

parameters.name: max_parallel_workers_per_gather
parameters._note: Match vCPU count. On a 2 vCPU instance there is no benefit to setting this higher - the second worker would compete with the leader process.

parameters.name: max_parallel_workers
parameters._note: Total parallel workers. Match vCPU count on m6g.large.

parameters.name: max_parallel_maintenance_workers
parameters._note: Workers for CREATE INDEX and VACUUM. 1 is sufficient at this data scale.

parameters.name: enable_partitionwise_join
parameters._note: Disabled - no partitioned tables at this scale. Re-enable via parameter group update when partitioning is added via migration.

parameters.name: enable_partitionwise_aggregate
parameters._note: Disabled - no partitioned tables at this scale. Re-enable when partitioning is added.

parameters.name: checkpoint_completion_target
parameters._note: Spreads checkpoint I/O over 90% of the checkpoint interval. Already the PG16 default but explicit is better.

parameters.name: checkpoint_timeout
parameters._note: 10 minutes between checkpoints. Slightly shorter than the large-scale recommendation since ETL loads are smaller and more frequent.

parameters.name: wal_buffers
parameters._note: 64MB WAL buffer (8192 × 8KB pages). Sufficient for nightly ETL batch loads at this scale.

parameters.name: default_statistics_target
parameters._note: Slightly above default (100) for better planner estimates on JSONB columns and multi-column predicates. Less aggressive than the large-scale version since tables are small.

parameters.name: random_page_cost
parameters._note: Close to seq_page_cost for SSD/NVMe-backed gp3 storage. Encourages index usage over sequential scans. Default of 4.0 assumes spinning disk.

parameters.name: effective_io_concurrency
parameters._note: 200 concurrent I/O ops for SSD storage. Enables bitmap heap scan prefetching.

parameters.name: pg_stat_statements.track
parameters._note: Track all statements including those inside functions. Essential for identifying slow ETL queries.

parameters.name: pg_stat_statements.max
parameters._note: Default 5000 is sufficient at this scale (3 division pipelines, moderate query variety).

parameters.name: log_min_duration_statement
parameters._note: Log queries taking longer than 2 seconds. More sensitive than the large-scale setting (5s) because at small data volumes, a 2-second query is a red flag worth investigating immediately.

parameters.name: log_temp_files
parameters._note: Log temp file creation over 10MB (lower threshold than large-scale version). At this data size, any spill to disk during a sort is a sign work_mem should be reviewed.

parameters.name: log_autovacuum_min_duration
parameters._note: Log autovacuum runs over 250ms. Lower threshold than large-scale - tables are small so any slow vacuum is worth knowing about.

parameters.name: autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor
parameters._note: Trigger VACUUM when 5% of rows are dead (default 20%). More aggressive on small tables prevents bloat from accumulating unnoticed.

parameters.name: autovacuum_analyze_scale_factor
parameters._note: Trigger ANALYZE when 2% of rows change. Keeps planner statistics current on staging tables that see nightly full refreshes.

parameters.name: idle_in_transaction_session_timeout
parameters._note: Kill sessions idle in a transaction for more than 2 minutes (120,000ms). Tighter than large-scale (5 min) because on a 2-vCPU instance a stuck transaction blocks VACUUM and causes visible performance degradation faster.

parameters.name: statement_timeout
parameters._note: No global timeout. Per-role timeouts are set in Section 8 of 01_datamart_init.sql: svc_bi_reader=300s, svc_analyst=1800s, ETL roles=unlimited.

parameters.name: lock_timeout
parameters._note: No global lock timeout. Per-role lock timeouts set in init SQL: 30s for ETL, 10s for readers. Global 0 allows the migration tool (Flyway/Alembic) to hold DDL locks without being killed.

parameters.name: track_io_timing
parameters._note: Enables I/O timing in EXPLAIN ANALYZE and pg_stat_statements. Marginal CPU overhead, essential for distinguishing CPU-bound from I/O-bound queries during tuning.

parameters.name: track_functions
parameters._note: Track function-level statistics. Useful once stored procedures are added for ETL transformations.

_upgrade_path._comment: When to revisit this parameter group
scale_triggers: fct_activities exceeds 1M rows → increase work_mem to 32768, consider partitioning
max_connections pressure → deploy RDS Proxy, then raise max_connections to 100
CPU consistently >60% → upgrade to db.m6g.xlarge (4 vCPU), raise max_parallel_workers to 4

_aws_cli_apply.create_group: aws rds create-db-parameter-group --db-parameter-group-name audiense-datamart-pg16 --db-parameter-group-family postgres16 --description 'Audiense data mart - m6g.large small scale'
